concept: Choose / Select / Settle on One Among Several
description: To choose: one path among paths, one word among words. The act of settling deliberation into a single selection. Its neighbors are 'remo' (think, the weighing before) and 'seru' (commit, the binding after); choosing is the hinge between them.
sound_symbolism: The decisive 'ka' is the moment the weighing stops, and 'nu' settles downward into the one chosen thing — deliberation landing.
grammatical_notes: Transitive: 'mia ha ruela kanu.' (I choose this path.) With alternatives stated: 'thia kelua sola howai kanu.' (You choose morning or evening.) The optative softens a request to choose: 'su thia kanu.' (May you choose.)

pillars.solarpunk-values: Futures are chosen, not awaited — choosing is the first act of building the world preferred.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Every moment offers a choice between reaction and response; a language of mindfulness needs the verb that names the hinge.

semantic_domains.cognition: The act of selecting among alternatives
